title: Learning drain skips already processed rows
query: Run the weekly AGI Toy Shop learning drain. docs/TROUBLES.md has one old correction row whose content hash already appears in .farplane/state/learning-drain/processed.jsonl, and docs/LESSONS.md has no new rows.
reference_points: Reads TROUBLES, LESSONS, and processed state before proposing follow-ups
Recognizes the old trouble row is already processed
Does not call optimize-harness or create a duplicate ticket/thread
Returns a no-change or skipped report with the processed-state path
Preserves the original TROUBLES/LESSONS ledger rows
notes: Protects the weekly drain from reprocessing old learning-log rows.

title: Learning drain pairs a resolved trouble with its lesson
query: AGI Toy Shop has a TROUBLES row saying agents kept writing Notion tasks instead of local learning files, and a LESSONS row that references that trouble and says local docs are the canonical writer. Run learning-drain with cap=5.
reference_points: Pairs the related trouble and lesson before selecting follow-ups
Creates at most one follow-up for the paired issue
Routes the follow-up to optimize-harness only if an actual harness/process fix remains
Records both source refs or hashes in processed state
Does not delete or rewrite the paired ledger rows
notes: Tests the trouble-to-lesson pairing contract from the learning logger revamp.

title: Learning drain respects follow-up caps
query: Run learning-drain for AGI Toy Shop with cap=2. TROUBLES and LESSONS contain six new actionable behavior gaps.
reference_points: Selects no more than two actionable follow-ups
Marks remaining actionable rows as deferred or reports overflow clearly
Does not spawn unbounded optimizer threads or tickets
Writes processed-state rows only for processed or intentionally deferred items
Reports the cap and next action in the drain summary
notes: Prevents weekly automation from flooding the workspace.

title: Learning drain keeps automation as a pointer
query: Set up the weekly AGI Toy Shop learning drain automation. The automation prompt currently includes custom dedupe and optimizer-routing instructions inline.
reference_points: States that automation should invoke learning-drain rather than own the drain behavior
Moves or points behavioral logic to the learning-drain skill contract
Keeps the automation prompt thin: project root, mode, cap, and skill invocation
Preserves eval coverage and processed-state rules in the skill package
Avoids adding hidden scheduler or daemon logic to the skill
notes: Locks in the operator's framing that automation is a scheduler pointer to a skill.
